title: Getting Started with ADS8192
text: JW26ADS8192 makes easy run Differential Gene Expression Analysis (via DESeq2) using RNA-seq data stored SummarizeExperiment objects.

title: Getting Started with ADS8192

not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> converting counts to integer mode #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> converting counts to integer mode #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> converting counts to integer mode #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> converting counts to integer mode #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.  # Step 2; Filter low expression genes se_filtered <- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 3: Run the DESeq2 pipeline to get differential gene expression results se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_filtered) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing  # Step 4: Run the log2_shrinkage function on the results of the DESeq2 function to create more reliable estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(se_dge) #> using 'apeglm' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite: #>     Zhu, A., Ibrahim, J.G., Love, M.I. (2018) Heavy-tailed prior distributions for #>     sequence count data: removing the noise and preserving large differences. #>     Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895  # Step 5: Create a volcano plot of the gene expression using the results obtained from the log2_shrinkage function example_se_volcano<- generate_volcano(se_dge_shrink)  # Step 6: Generate the regulation summary of the genes DESeq2_gene_reg_summary<- gene_regulation_summary(se_dge_shrink)  # Step 7: Export final results example_se_exports<- export_outputs(res_df = se_dge_shrink, summary_df = DESeq2_gene_reg_summary, filtering_diag = example_se_filtering_assessment, volcano = example_se_volcano, output_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "de_output") ) #> Export complete. Files saved: #> /tmp/RtmpMTQ5k4/de_output/de_results.tsv #> /tmp/RtmpMTQ5k4/de_output/de_summary.tsv #> /tmp/RtmpMTQ5k4/de_output/filtering_diagnostics.tsv #> /tmp/RtmpMTQ5k4/de_output/volcano_plot.pdf #> /tmp/RtmpMTQ5k4/de_output/volcano_plot.png

title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
text: JW26ADS8192 provides tool preforms Differential Gene Analysis SummarizedExperiment Objects.

title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("wilsonjewel27/JW26ADS8192")

title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: library(JW26ADS8192)  #load example data data(example_se)  #Assess the 'minimun gene count' filtering threshold to determine the best threshold. example_se_filtering_assessment<- determine_filter_threshold(se_ln = example_se,count_thresholds = c(0, 1, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500), assay_name = "counts", ref_level = "Tconv", group_var = "cell_type", p_threshold = 0.05)  #Filter out the low expression gene se_filtered <- filter_low_exp_genes(se_ln = example_se, min_count_per_group = 10, assay_name = "counts")  #Run the DESeq2 pipeline se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_ln = se_filtered, group_var = "cell_type", ref_level = "Tconv")  #Apply the log2fold-change shrinkage for more reliable effect-size estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(dds = se_dge, shrinkage = "apeglm")  #Summarize the number of upregulated, down regulated and non-signficant genes in a dataframe DESeq2_gene_reg_summary<- gene_regulation_summary(res_df = se_dge_shrink, p_threshold = 0.05, fc_threshold =  0.5)  #Generate the volcano plot corresponging to the gene regulation summary dataframe example_se_volcano<- generate_volcano(res_df = se_dge_shrink, fc_threshold =  0.5, xlab = "log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)", set_title = "Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv", p_threshold = 0.05)  #Export the filtering threhold assessment, differential expression results and Expression-Count summary to TSV files & the volcano plot to pdf and png example_se_exports<- export_outputs(res_df = se_dge_shrink, summary_df = DESeq2_gene_reg_summary, filtering_diag = example_se_filtering_assessment, volcano = example_se_volcano, output_dir = file.path("ADD/YOUR/PATH", "de_output") )

what: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
title: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
text: Provides tool differential gene analysis lymph node regulatory T cells (Tregs) conventional T cells (Tconv) isolated four mouse tissues (fat, liver, lymph node, skin). Tool includes functions (1) filtering low expression gene counts, (2) preforms DESeq2 differential expression analysis, (3) applies log2fold-change shrinkage DESeq2 results, (4) summarizes number //non-significant genes given thresholds, (5) computes indepdenpt filtering diagonsitics, (6) visualizes results volcano plot, (7) exportsthe results TSV files.

title: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
text: Maintainer: Wilson Jewel jewel.wilson@stjude.org (ORCID)

text: SummarizedExperiment subset genes greater n counts

title: Filter genes with low expression — filter_low_exp_genes
code: data(example_se)  # Step 1; Filter low expression genes se_filtered <- filter_low_exp_genes(se_ln = example_se, min_count_per_group = 10, assay_name = "counts") #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch

what: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
title: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
text: Summarize number significantly non-significant(ns)//genes given thresholds

title: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
code: gene_regulation_summary(res_df, p_threshold = 0.05, fc_threshold = 0.5)

title: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
text: res_df Dataframe log2fold-changed shrinkage applied ds p_threshold Significance threshold adjusted p-value (default: 0.05) fc_threshold fold change threshold (default = 0.5)

title: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
text: Summary non-significant(ns)//genes

title: Summarize the number of significantly non-significant(ns)/up/down genes at given thresholds — gene_regulation_summary
code: data(example_se)  # Step 1; Filter low expression genes se_filtered<- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 2: Run the DESeq2 pipeline to get differential gene expression results  se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_filtered) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing  # Step 3: Run the log2_shrinkage function on the results of the DESeq2 function to create more reliable estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(se_dge) #> using 'apeglm' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite: #>     Zhu, A., Ibrahim, J.G., Love, M.I. (2018) Heavy-tailed prior distributions for #>     sequence count data: removing the noise and preserving large differences. #>     Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895  #Step 4: Generate the regulation summary of the genes DESeq2_gene_reg_summary<- gene_regulation_summary(res_df = se_dge_shrink, p_threshold = 0.05, fc_threshold =  0.5)

what: Visualize results as a volcano plot — generate_volcano

code: generate_volcano(   res_df,   fc_threshold = 0.5,   xlab = "log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)",   set_title = "Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv",   p_threshold = 0.05 )

title: Visualize results as a volcano plot — generate_volcano
text: res_df Dataframe log2fold-changed shrinkage applied ds fc_threshold fold change threshold(default: 0.5) xlab label x-axis set_title title plot p_threshold threshold defines gene's significance (default: 0.05)

title: Visualize results as a volcano plot — generate_volcano
text: volcano pot significant genes highlighted

title: Visualize results as a volcano plot — generate_volcano
code: data(example_se)  # Step 1; Filter low expression genes se_filtered<- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 2: Run the DESeq2 pipeline to get differential gene expression results  se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_filtered) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing  # Step 3: Run the log2_shrinkage function on the results of the DESeq2 function to create more reliable estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(se_dge) #> using 'apeglm' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite: #>     Zhu, A., Ibrahim, J.G., Love, M.I. (2018) Heavy-tailed prior distributions for #>     sequence count data: removing the noise and preserving large differences. #>     Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895  # Step 4: Create a volcano plot of the gene expression using the results obtained from the log2_shrinkage function example_se_volcano<- generate_volcano(res_df = se_dge_shrink, fc_threshold =  0.5, xlab = "log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)", set_title = "Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv", p_threshold = 0.05)

title: Apply log2fold-change shrinkage for more reliable effect-size estimates — log2_shrinkage
text: dds results 'DESeq2_function' shrinkage estimator used assess glm coefficeints (default: "apeglm")

title: Apply log2fold-change shrinkage for more reliable effect-size estimates — log2_shrinkage
text: Dataframe log2fold-changed shrinkage applied ds

title: Apply log2fold-change shrinkage for more reliable effect-size estimates — log2_shrinkage
code: data(example_se)  # Step 1; Filter low expression genes se_filtered<- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 2: Run the DESeq2 pipeline to get differential gene expression results  se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_filtered) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing  # Step 3: Run the log2_shrinkage function on the results of the DESeq2 function to create more reliable estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(dds = se_dge, shrinkage = "apeglm") #> using 'apeglm' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite: #>     Zhu, A., Ibrahim, J.G., Love, M.I. (2018) Heavy-tailed prior distributions for #>     sequence count data: removing the noise and preserving large differences. #>     Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895

text: results Differential Gene Analysis cell_type

title: Preform Differential Gene Analysis — run_DESeq2
code: data(example_se)  # Step 1; Filter low expression genes se_filtered<- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 500  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 2: Run the DESeq2 pipeline se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_ln = se_filtered, group_var = "cell_type", ref_level = "Tconv") #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing
